scenarios.title: Delta — add waitlists to the course platform

top_gaps: How an end_user is created during submission — UC-001 requires a not-null end_user_id but no flow specifies find-or-create-by-email semantics
The per-role permission matrix for owner/admin/agent — 'appropriate permissions' is never defined, yet triage (UC-002) and release creation (UC-005) assume role gating
Organization and member onboarding — entirely absent from flows and use cases despite 'membership and roles' being in scope

Contradictory data assertions / 4 alignment errors — UC-006 expects shipped in usecases.md but shipping in usecases.sql; UC-003/DA-3 expects a uniqueness error against a single-column design that cannot raise one; plan cites a 'Status' term missing from the glossary
Provider reputation has no home in the data model despite being an explicit policy (FL-006), a use-case step (UC-006), and a core brief value prop — agent must invent storage and computation.

Data assertions contradict their acceptance criteria on enforcement — most starkly UC-006 (AC-2: reject rating a non-completed Booking; DA-2: insert succeeds, rowcount=1) — leaving the agent unsure whether the rule exists and whether to enforce it in DB or app code.
Business-rule constraints the brief/ACs require are missing from the schema: stars 1–5 range (no CHECK), 'rate only completed bookings', and 'cannot accept a declined request'.
Payment amount derivation is unspecified (seeded as a literal), so the agent must guess hourly_rate × duration or similar.

Capacity enforcement: when is a course 'full', and is direct enrollment rejected at capacity? This drives the entire enroll-vs-waitlist branch yet has no acceptance criterion — despite being the brief's core problem.

Offer expiry: the duration of expires_at is unstated, and what happens when an offer expires (re-offer to the next waitlisted student?) is undefined — the 'expired' offer status and 'offered' entry status are orphaned by every flow.
Waitlist entry terminal status: the status enum (pending/offered/declined) has no 'accepted/fulfilled' state, so the entry's fate after UC-005 acceptance is left to the agent to guess.
Inconsistency: UC-005's already-enrolled rejection is specified as 'error ~ check' in usecases.md but enforced via the unique (student_id, course_id) constraint ('error ~ unique') in usecases.sql.
Status-transition semantics: enforced lifecycle state machine vs. any-valid-status free set (brief's arrow vs. UC-008 enum-only validation)
Owner's initial is_active state at org registration (default false vs. UC-001 making the owner the active first member)
Cross-org and single-owner / removed-member business rules are specified in ACs but not schema-enforced nor covered by data assertions — builder must implement and self-test them
How is a booking's price determined on acceptance — computed from hourly_rate × window duration, or supplied as an agreed amount? (UC-005 'agreed price' vs. hard-coded DA value)
Authorization/ownership is entirely unmodeled: who may accept/decline (the service's provider?), complete and rate (the booking's customer?) — no AC enforces it
Status-transition and rating-eligibility enforcement is described only in DBML Notes; the trigger/CHECK implementation that makes the 'error ~ check' assertions pass must be authored from scratch
No browse/list/query use case despite 'browse providers / compare rates' being the core problem — only mutations are specified
UC-008 names a 'Payment Processor' actor while the brief rules out processor integration — what actually drives payment_status, and who is allowed to?
How a 'seat offer' is represented between drop and accept — waitlist_entries has no offered state and there is no offer entity, yet UC-005/UC-006 and FL-005/FL-006 depend on it
Whether enrollment must be rejected when the course is at capacity — the brief's stated 'double bookings' problem implies it, but no use case enforces it
FL-001's 'Recount course seats' policy and 'Course seats recounted' event have no use case and no place in the data model (no seat-count column or view)
Authorization: hierarchical owner/admin/agent privilege model asserted in the brief but no UC, AC, or live test enforces role-based denial — the agent must invent the authz layer.
Transport/API surface unspecified (no endpoints, payloads, REST-vs-GraphQL), so the agent picks the external interface unilaterally.
No use case for browsing/listing/filtering the inbox (only single-item history view) — primary read/query/pagination surface left to invention.
Physical end-user submission channel (widget/public endpoint/email) named as the intake path but left out of scope, so ingestion wiring is undefined.

Rating-on-completed-only is mandated by the brief as a hard data-layer invariant but the schema never enforces booking status on rating insert — agent must add a trigger/CHECK or knowingly ship a weaker model.
Booking lifecycle transitions are unguarded: nothing prevents requested→completed or backward moves, though UC-007 assumes 'a confirmed Booking' is what gets completed.
No authorization model: which actor may accept/decline a booking (must it be the service's provider?) and who may complete it — UPDATEs carry no ownership check.
Authentication is entirely unspecified for a two-sided marketplace (only email + display_name).
Price at acceptance: computed from hourly_rate × window or entered by the provider, and is it overridable?
Offer expiry is completely unspecified — no expires_at/timeout column, no rule for what happens to an unaccepted offer, no re-offer to the next waiting entry.
Capacity/'full' is narrative only: occupancy is never defined and UC-001 enroll has no capacity guard, so 'a course is full' (UC-004) is unenforceable as written.

Flows vs. use cases contradict on causation: FL-005/FL-006 make drop->offer and accept->enroll automatic policies, but the use cases and usecases.sql implement them as independent manual registrar UPDATEs/INSERTs.
Lifecycle transitions are unguarded — nothing requires offer only from waiting or accept only from offered, and accept->enroll is neither atomic nor links the new enrollment to the waitlist entry.
